title: Campaign
description:
A campaign represents a discrete set of communications for a client.
    Messages sent as part of a campaign can be tracked and reported on together, and are isolated from each other for dispatch throttling purposes.
    For example, a client might have separate campaigns for appointment reminders, health check invitations, and test results.
    Queue configuration is aggregated per channel, and GOV.UK Notify integration settings are embedded per environment.

slug.title: Campaign Slug
slug.description: Client-unique human-readable identifier used to distinguish campaigns for the same client.

status.title: EnvironmentStatus
status.description: Indicates whether the configuration is in draft, enabled in INT, enabled in PROD, or disabled in all environments. `PROD` implies the configuration has also been promoted through INT.

clientId.title: Client ID Reference
clientId.description: Reference to a Client by its unique identifier

default.title: Default Campaign
default.description:
Indicates whether this campaign is the default for a client (i.e. not specific to a purpose).
      Each client can have only one default campaign per environment.
      It will be used for any messages sent without a specific campaign, such as those using global routing configurations.

govukNotifyConfigurations.description: Environment-scoped GOV.UK Notify account settings used by the campaign.

items.title: GovuknotifyAccount
items.description: Environment-scoped GOV.UK Notify configuration for a campaign.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: The target environment for an environment-scoped configuration entry.

apiKeyParameterPath.title: GOV.UK Notify API Key Parameter Store Path
apiKeyParameterPath.description: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store path containing the GOV.UK Notify API key for the target environment.

senderId.description: Optional GOV.UK Notify sender identifier for the target environment.

queues.description:
Each channel (e.g. email, sms) that this campaign will use to send messages has an associated queue.
    Each queue may also have associated throttling limits to control the rate of message dispatch.

items.title: Queue
items.description: Campaign queue configuration for a single channel, including per-queue throttling limits and active schedule windows.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: A channel with which a message can be sent

maxRequestsPerSecond.description: Maximum number of requests that may be dispatched per second while the queue throttling rules are active.

maxRequestsPerPeriod.description: Maximum number of requests that may be dispatched during a single quota period while the queue throttling rules are active.

periodSeconds.description: Length of the quota period, in seconds, used with maxRequestsPerPeriod.

periodOffset.description: Offset in seconds applied to the start of each quota period calculation.

schedule.description: Schedule periods during which queue throttling is active. The quota applies whenever the current time falls within any defined schedule period.

items.title: QueueSchedulePeriod
items.description: A scheduled time window during which queue throttling is active. Multiple schedule periods may overlap.

daysOfWeek.description: Days of the week on which this queue schedule period is active.

items.title: DayOfWeek
items.description: Day of the week on which a queue schedule period applies.

startTime.title: Start Time
startTime.description: Inclusive start time for the active schedule period, in HH:MM 24-hour format.

endTime.title: End Time
endTime.description: Exclusive end time for the active schedule period, in HH:MM 24-hour format.

queueNamePrefixOverride.description: Optional prefix override used when generating the queue name for this campaign channel.
